name: Amazon SQS API
what_it_does: Amazon Simple Queue Service — a fully managed message queuing service for decoupling and scaling microservices, distributed systems, and serverless applications.
use_cases: Decoupling microservices with async message passing
Buffering writes for downstream systems that can't keep up with bursts
Fan-out messaging when combined with SNS for multiple subscribers
Dead letter queue handling for failed message processing
Agent task queuing for distributed AI workflow execution
not_for: Real-time pub/sub with many consumers (use SNS or Kafka)
Message streaming with replay/rewind semantics (use Kinesis or Kafka)
best_when: You're building on AWS and need reliable, scalable point-to-point message queuing with minimal operational overhead.
avoid_when: You need message streaming with replay, complex routing, or pub/sub to many consumers.

auth.notes: AWS Signature Version 4 (SigV4) for all API calls. IAM roles recommended for EC2/Lambda; access keys for external. Least-privilege IAM policies should restrict to specific queue ARNs.

pricing.notes: Very affordable. Free tier covers most small workloads. Data transfer costs apply for cross-region. FIFO queues cost slightly more than standard queues.

performance.rate_limits: Unlimited throughput for standard queues; 3000 msg/s for FIFO

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: AWS-standard error XML/JSON with error codes and descriptions. Errors are well-documented and consistent across AWS services.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: FIFO queues support message deduplication IDs for exactly-once delivery. Standard queues provide at-least-once delivery (duplicates possible).
known_agent_gotchas: Standard queues deliver at-least-once — agents must handle duplicate messages with idempotent processing
Messages have a 15-minute maximum visibility timeout — long-running agent tasks must extend it
Long polling (up to 20 seconds) is strongly recommended over short polling for cost efficiency
Message size limit is 256KB — large payloads need S3 reference pattern (SQS Extended Client Library)
Dead letter queues need explicit setup — failed messages silently drop without them
FIFO throughput limit (3000 msg/s with batching) can be a bottleneck at scale

security_score_components.security_notes: IAM-based auth with least-privilege queue ARN scoping. Managed Identity support via EC2/Lambda roles avoids static keys. Server-side encryption with KMS. VPC endpoint support for private network access. FedRAMP High, HIPAA, PCI-DSS certified.
